name: DayZ
author: ops@refx.example
description: Bohemia Interactive's open-world zombie survival. SteamCMD-installed dedicated server; runs well as a native process for mod loading.

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -uo pipefail
mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server
# Locate or bootstrap steamcmd (the parkervcp/steamcmd image does NOT ship it
# on PATH; eggs are expected to fetch it themselves).
if command -v steamcmd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  STEAMCMD=steamcmd
else
  echo "[steam] bootstrapping steamcmd"
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
  curl -sSL https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz | tar zxf - -C /mnt/server/steamcmd
  chmod +x /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh /mnt/server/steamcmd/linux32/steamcmd 2>/dev/null || true
  STEAMCMD=/mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh
fi
# Per-server Steam home — the CUSTOMER's mod-account machine-auth lives with
# the server's own volume.
SERVER_HOME=/mnt/server/.steamhome
mkdir -p "$SERVER_HOME"
# The HOST game-download account uses a node-level home (REFX_NODE_STEAM_HOME,
# mounted by the agent) so its Steam Guard machine-auth persists across servers
# — a code is only needed once per node. Falls back to the per-server home on
# older agents that don't provide it.
GAME_HOME="${REFX_NODE_STEAM_HOME:-$SERVER_HOME}"
mkdir -p "$GAME_HOME" 2>/dev/null || GAME_HOME="$SERVER_HOME"
# BASE GAME download uses the HOST's game-download account (Admin → Settings → Steam).
if [ -n "${STEAM_GAME_USERNAME:-}" ] && [ -n "${STEAM_GAME_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then
  GLOGIN=(+login "$STEAM_GAME_USERNAME" "$STEAM_GAME_PASSWORD")
  [ -n "${STEAM_GAME_GUARD:-}" ] && GLOGIN+=("$STEAM_GAME_GUARD")
else
  GLOGIN=(+login anonymous)
fi
HOME="$GAME_HOME" "$STEAMCMD" +force_install_dir /mnt/server "${GLOGIN[@]}" +app_update 223350 validate +quit || echo "[steam] base install reported an error (login / ownership / Steam Guard?)"
# WORKSHOP MODS download with the HOST game-download account (Admin -> Settings ->
# Steam) — it owns the game, so customers never enter Steam credentials.
WS_APP="${WORKSHOP_APP_ID:-221100}"
if [ -n "${WORKSHOP_ITEMS:-}" ]; then
  # Download every item in a SINGLE steamcmd session (one login) rather than
  # relaunching + re-logging-in per item — far faster and avoids Steam login
  # rate limits on big collections.
  DL=(+force_install_dir /mnt/server "${GLOGIN[@]}")
  for id in $WORKSHOP_ITEMS; do DL+=(+workshop_download_item "$WS_APP" "$id" validate); done
  DL+=(+quit)
  echo "[workshop] downloading:$WORKSHOP_ITEMS"
  HOME="$GAME_HOME" "$STEAMCMD" "${DL[@]}" || echo "[workshop] one or more downloads reported an error (is the game-download account set + owning the game?)"
  # Post-process each item: lowercase (Linux is case-sensitive), symlink, keys.
  for id in $WORKSHOP_ITEMS; do
    src="/mnt/server/steamapps/workshop/content/$WS_APP/$id"
    [ -d "$src" ] || { echo "[workshop] $id not downloaded"; continue; }
    find "$src" -depth | while read -r p; do
      b="$(basename "$p")"; lb="$(printf '%s' "$b" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
      [ "$b" = "$lb" ] || { pd="$(dirname "$p")"; [ -e "$pd/$lb" ] || mv "$p" "$pd/$lb"; }
    done
    ln -sfn "steamapps/workshop/content/$WS_APP/$id" "/mnt/server/@ws_$id"
    mkdir -p /mnt/server/keys
    find "$src" -iname '*.bikey' -exec cp -f {} /mnt/server/keys/ \; 2>/dev/null || true
  done
fi
if [ ! -f /mnt/server/serverDZ.cfg ]; then cp /mnt/server/serverDZ.server.cfg /mnt/server/serverDZ.cfg 2>/dev/null || true; fi
# Write the ReFx launcher that runs the server and streams its RPT log to the
# panel console (DayZ logs detail to .RPT/.ADM files, not stdout).
cat > /mnt/server/refx-run.sh <<'RUNEOF'
#!/bin/bash
# ReFx launcher for DayZ: runs the dedicated server AND streams its RPT log to
# stdout, because DayZ writes its detailed output to .RPT/.ADM files in the
# profiles dir rather than to stdout. This makes the panel console useful.
set -u
cd /home/container 2>/dev/null || cd "$(dirname "$0")"
PROFILES="$PWD/profiles"
mkdir -p "$PROFILES"

ARGS=(-config=serverDZ.cfg -port="${SERVER_PORT:-2302}" -profiles="$PROFILES" -dologs -adminlog -netlog -freezecheck)
# Build the -mod list: any user-specified MODS plus the enabled Workshop items
# the panel downloaded (symlinked as @ws_<id> by the install step).
MODLIST="${MODS:-}"
for id in ${WORKSHOP_ITEMS:-}; do
  m="@ws_$id"
  [ -e "$m" ] || continue
  case ";$MODLIST;" in *";$m;"*) ;; *) MODLIST="${MODLIST:+$MODLIST;}$m";; esac
done
[ -n "$MODLIST" ] && ARGS+=(-mod="$MODLIST")

SRV=
shutdown() { [ -n "$SRV" ] && kill -TERM "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; }
trap shutdown TERM INT

./DayZServer "${ARGS[@]}" &
SRV=$!

# Follow the newest RPT to stdout, re-targeting when DayZ rotates the file.
(
  last=
  while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do
    f="$(find "$PROFILES" -type f -iname '*.rpt' -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -n1 | cut -d' ' -f2-)"
    if [ -n "$f" ] && [ "$f" != "$last" ]; then
      last="$f"
      echo "[refx] streaming DayZ log: ${f##*/}"
      tail -n +1 -F "$f" 2>/dev/null &
    fi
    sleep 2
  done
) &

# Wait for the server to exit, surviving the SIGTERM that triggers graceful stop.
while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do wait "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; done
RUNEOF
chmod +x /mnt/server/refx-run.sh
echo 'DayZ install complete.'

variables.description: Hostname shown in the DayZ server browser.
Extra mod folders to load, semicolon-separated (e.g. @CF;@VPPAdminTools). Workshop items added on the Workshop tab are loaded automatically.
Password protecting BattlEye RCON admin access.

longDescription: Hardcore open-world zombie survival. Host persistent DayZ servers with custom loot, mods and rules.
